qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing requestComplexity defaults enable unauthenticated DoS via deeply nested GraphQL and REST queries
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns missing requestComplexity defaults and unbounded GraphQL/REST query complexity, while the advisory concerns PostgreSQL SQL injection through improper escaping of dot-notation sub-field values used with sort, distinct, or where query parameters.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The only final qualifying finding reports disabled request-complexity limits causing denial of service; it does not describe the PostgreSQL dot-notation field escaping flaw that enables SQL injection through sort, distinct, or where parameters.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-qpr4-jrj4-6f27 describes SQL injection in parse-server PostgreSQL deployments caused by improper escaping of dot-notation sub-field values, especially with the sort query parameter.
Final session output lists dos-missing-request-complexity-defaults as the only qualifying_vuln folder and ssrf-webhook-urls as non_qualifying_vuln.

dos-missing-request-complexity-defaults/vulnerability_report.json identifies Config.validateRequestComplexity(), GraphQL query complexity checks, REST subquery depth checks, and include complexity checks as the vulnerable paths, not PostgreSQL dot-notation field escaping or SQL generation.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No qualifying finding matched the advisory root cause, so runtime tiering is not applicable.
